data.title: U.S. Border Chief Reaffirms Commitment to Large-Scale Deportations
data.description: The U.S. Border Chief, Homan, has reiterated the administration's consistent stance since U.S. President Donald Trump took office on January 20. According to Jin10, Homan emphasized that the focus will be on conducting large-scale deportation operations, prioritizing the arrest of criminals and those posing threats to public safety.

data.title: RINA Accountants & Advisors Settles 2022 Data Breach for $400,000
data.description: RINA Accountants & Advisors has agreed to a $400,000 settlement to address a data breach that occurred in 2022, which exposed sensitive information such as Social Security Numbers. According to NS3.AI, affected individuals can claim up to $10,000 for documented economic losses. Additionally, they will receive three years of credit monitoring and identity theft protection, which includes $1 million in insurance coverage. Claims and objections must be submitted by March 24, with the final settlement approval set for May 14.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Infrastructure Expands Beyond Speculation to Wall Street Integration
data.description:
Phyrex posted on X. The cryptocurrency landscape is evolving from mere speculation to becoming a foundational infrastructure for Wall Street and major payment companies. Financial institutions are not just jumping on the bandwagon; they are expanding their workforce across various roles, including product development, business development, trading, backend engineering, and blockchain development. This comprehensive hiring strategy indicates their commitment to building a long-term system.

Major players in the payment sector, such as Visa, Mastercard, and American Express, are involved, alongside banking giants like JPMorgan, Citi, and Morgan Stanley. Asset management firms like BlackRock and consulting companies like EY are also participating. These institutions aim to create an integrated business model encompassing payments, banking, asset management, and compliance services. The goal is to incorporate on-chain assets into traditional financial frameworks for settlement, custody, risk management, and compliance.

The path to compliance is gradually taking shape, with Wall Street actively recruiting talent, securing positions, and developing products. They are betting on a future where asset issuance, settlement, and capital flows increasingly move towards regulated on-chain and crypto-financial systems.

data.title: The US XRP spot ETF saw a net inflow of $16.79 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, XRP spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $16.79 million yesterday (January 30th, Eastern Time). The XRP spot ETF with the largest net inflow yesterday (January 30th, Eastern Time) was the 21Shares XRP ETF (TOXR), with a single-day net inflow of $8.19 million and a historical total net inflow of $419 million. This was followed by the Bitwise XRP ETF (XRP), with a single-day net inflow of $3.91 million and a historical total net inflow of $338 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of XRP spot ETFs was $1.19 billion, with an XRP net asset ratio of 1.13%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $1.18 billion.

data.title: JPMorgan Chase: Bitcoin futures are oversold, while gold and silver futures have entered overbought territory.
data.description: JPMorgan Chase stated that momentum indicators show Bitcoin futures are oversold, while gold and silver futures are overbought, reflecting significant institutional and momentum traders' holdings in precious metals. The bank noted that retail investors have shifted from Bitcoin to gold and silver since August, and pointed to a long-term price expectation of $8,000 to $8,500 per ounce based on increasing allocations by private investors and central banks. (The Block)

data.title: JPMorgan Highlights Bitcoin Futures Oversold, Gold and Silver Overbought
data.description:
JPMorgan has indicated that momentum indicators show Bitcoin futures are oversold, while gold and silver futures are overbought. According to Odaily, this reflects significant holdings in precious metals by institutional and momentum traders.

The bank noted that since August, retail investors have shifted their focus from Bitcoin to gold and silver. It also highlighted that based on increasing allocations by private investors and central banks, the long-term price expectation for gold is between $8,000 and $8,500 per ounce.

data.title: Wintermute Founder Criticizes Misinterpretation of Market Volatility
data.description: Wintermute founder Evgeny Gaevoy expressed his views on social media on January 31. According to BlockBeats, Gaevoy emphasized the importance of public figures exercising caution in their statements. He clarified that the market fluctuations on October 10 were not due to a 'software glitch' but were instead triggered by macroeconomic news during a low-liquidity Friday evening, leading to a flash crash in a highly leveraged market. Gaevoy acknowledged the general dislike for bear markets, especially when all asset classes except crypto are rising. He criticized the tendency to scapegoat, stating that blaming a single trading platform is not an honest assessment of the situation.

data.title: Arthur Hayes: Warsh's appointment as Fed Chairman will not stop "money printing," and pullbacks are still buying opportunities.
data.description: BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes stated in an article on the X platform that even if former Federal Reserve Governor Kevin Warsh were to become Fed Chairman, it's unlikely that monetary easing would truly cease. This is because Warsh voted in favor of the first and second rounds of quantitative easing during his tenure as a Fed governor, only publicly opposing certain policies after leaving the Fed and when those decisions no longer had a real impact. The Fed continues to "print money" because genuine fiscal tightening would lead to the collapse of the entire financial system. In this context, regardless of who leads the Fed, they will ultimately choose to continue releasing liquidity. He bluntly stated, "Warsh would also print money," and advised the market to "BTFD" (buy on dips) during pullbacks.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum, and Solana Long Positions Face Significant Losses
data.description: Onchain Lens posted on X. In the current bearish market, the long positions in Bitcoin (BTC), Ethereum (ETH), and Solana (SOL) are experiencing substantial floating losses. The total loss has now surpassed $25 million, with the Bitcoin OG (10/11) position alone facing a floating loss of $109 million.

data.title: A victim lost 4,556 ETH, worth $12.25 million, due to accidentally copying a corrupted transaction record address.
data.description: According to Scam Sniffer | Web3 Anti-Scam monitoring, 10 hours ago, a victim lost 4,556 ETH, worth $12.25 million, after copying the wrong address from a tainted transaction history.

data.title: U.S. Government Shutdown Highlights Prediction Market Challenges
data.description: The potential U.S. government shutdown, pending House approval of a funding bill, has highlighted challenges within prediction markets such as Polymarket and Kalshi. According to NS3.AI, these platforms face limitations in contract design and specificity, affecting the accuracy of bets during political events. The situation underscores the importance of clearly framed questions in prediction markets.

data.title: Bitcoin's Future Outlook: Potential Consolidation and Long-Term Growth
data.description: Bitcoin is anticipated to consolidate within a $75,000 to $100,000 range during the first half of the year, according to NS3.AI. Bitwise CIO Matt Hougan suggests this stability will be influenced by regulatory clarity and the digestion of macroeconomic risks. He predicts a potential breakout later in the year as institutional understanding and broader adoption of Bitcoin improve. Looking further ahead, Hougan forecasts that Bitcoin could reach approximately $6.5 million per coin over the next 20 years. This growth is expected to be driven primarily by global debt expansion and currency devaluation, rather than accelerated adoption.

data.title: Bloomberg: Nomura's European business turned to a loss due to the decline in crypto assets and has tightened its management of positions and risk exposure.
data.description: A Nomura Holdings executive said the company has strengthened risk controls on its cryptocurrency business after its European operations turned unprofitable due to setbacks in the digital asset market. Chief Financial Officer Hiroyuki Moriuchi said on Friday's conference call reviewing quarterly results: "We have tightened management of our positions and risk exposure to mitigate short-term profit volatility." He added that Japan's largest brokerage firm has reduced its virtual currency holdings but remains committed to the long-term development of its cryptocurrency business. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Sees Mixed Performance with Notable Gains and Losses
data.description:
On January 31, several cryptocurrencies experienced significant fluctuations in their market values. According to PANews, the top gainers of the day included NOT, which is currently priced at $0.000504, marking a 4.80% increase. STX followed with a price of $0.291, rising by 2.46%, while STRK is now at $0.0616, up by 2.29%. JUP and FLOW also saw gains, with JUP at $0.207, increasing by 1.82%, and FLOW at $0.0665, up by 1.49%.

Conversely, some cryptocurrencies faced declines. AXS is now priced at $1.953, experiencing a decrease of 8.86%. ZRO followed with a price of $1.822, down by 6.95%. ENJ, ILV, and HBAR also saw declines, with ENJ at $0.0269, dropping by 3.37%, ILV at $4.903, down by 3.06%, and HBAR at $0.0984, decreasing by 2.33%.
